old_claim.note: Electronic Health Records provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from point_of_care_testing to electronic_health_records; POCT is defined by near-patient testing and rapid turnaround, not by record software.

source_shape.source_locator: Introduction defining POCT as clinical laboratory testing conducted at or near the site of patient care with rapid turnaround.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines point-of-care testing as near-patient clinical laboratory testing that brings testing to the patient for rapid results.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This refutes EHR as a method prerequisite because the defining capability is portable or near-patient testing, while EHRs are result-management and workflow systems.

ontology_before: Point-of-care testing depended on electronic health records, mixing a diagnostic testing modality with later clinical information infrastructure.
ontology_after: Point-of-care testing is modeled as near-patient diagnostic testing; electronic records may support reporting or integration but are not a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: point_of_care_testing no longer directly depends on electronic_health_records.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to connected POCT result-management platforms rather than point-of-care testing generally.
A source showed early or general POCT cannot function without EHR infrastructure.
A later graph split introduced a narrower connected_poCT_platforms node that intentionally depends on EHR integration.
short_reason: Point-of-care testing is near-patient diagnostic testing, not an EHR-dependent technology.
